perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is valid and binding on theparent and/or holding and/or subsidiary(s) and/or associate(s) and/or affiliate and/or related companies and/or entities, directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is valid and binding only on the directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates a contradiction by limiting the binding nature of the agreement only to directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title, and permitted assigns, and excluding the parent, holding, subsidiary, associate, affiliate, and related companies/entities. This contradicts the original text that explicitly includes these related entities. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding which entities are bound by the agreement and can lead to disputes over liability and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is valid and binding only on the directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the scope of who is bound by the agreement, excluding parent, holding, subsidiary, associate, affiliate, and related companies/entities. This directly contradicts the provided 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Agreement is only valid and binding on the directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties, creating a clear inconsistency and uncertainty about which entities are bound by the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it provides conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean the said Submissions and each concept, idea, game-play mechanic, set design, business model, and/or other element contained therein and any and all other proprietary and/or any other information and/or data which is provided and/or obtained hereunder weather in relation to the submission and/or otherwise, whether in writing, pictorially, in machine readable form, orally or by observation during their interactions/discussions, in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, including but not limited to, all intangible and tangible information, documents, data, papers, statements, any business/customer information and trade secrets relating to its business practices in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, and will form a part of the proprietary and confidential information weather disclosed by the Disclosing and/or Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean the said Submissions and each concept, idea, game-play mechanic, set design, business model, and/or other element contained therein and any and all other proprietary and/or any other information and/or data which is provided and/or obtained hereunder weather in relation to the submission and/or otherwise, whether in writing, pictorially, in machine readable form, orally or by observation during their interactions/discussions, in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, including but not limited to, all intangible and tangible information, documents, data, papers, statements, any business/customer information and trade secrets relating to its business practices in connection with the Purpose or otherwise. However, only the information clearly marked as 'Confidential' will be considered as confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds a contradictory condition to the definition of "Confidential Information". Initially, the definition is broad and encompasses all information related to the submission. However, the added sentence restricts this definition by stating that only information clearly marked as 'Confidential' will be considered confidential. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the clause that encompasses a wide variety of information regardless of whether it's explicitly marked as such. It creates confusion over what information the Receiving Party is obligated to keep confidential and introduces a loophole for disclosing information that is not explicitly marked as 'Confidential'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean the said Submissions and each concept, idea, game-play mechanic, set design, business model, and/or other element contained therein and any and all other proprietary and/or any other information and/or data which is provided and/or obtained hereunder weather in relation to the submission and/or otherwise, whether in writing, pictorially, in machine readable form, orally or by observation during their interactions/discussions, in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, including but not limited to, all intangible and tangible information, documents, data, papers, statements, any business/customer information and trade secrets relating to its business practices in connection with the Purpose or otherwise. However, only the information clearly marked as 'Confidential' will be considered as confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a contradictory condition to the definition of "Confidential Information". The original definition is broad, encompassing all information related to the submission. However, the added sentence restricts this definition by stating that only information clearly marked as 'Confidential' will be considered confidential. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the clause that encompasses a wide variety of information regardless of whether it's explicitly marked as such, causing an inconsistency as defined in Perturbation Definitions 5. The contradicted text is located in the same location as the changed text, but the change is what creates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive in perpetuity after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive for a period of one (1) year after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates a contradiction by changing the survival period of confidentiality obligations from "in perpetuity" to "one (1) year". This direct contradiction introduces significant uncertainty regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. The enforcement will become uncertain since the other is stating that it will last forever, and the second clause is saying that it will only last one year, therefore this makes this contract disputable in the court of law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive for a period of one (1) year after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality obligations from 'in perpetuity' to 'one (1) year'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that confidentiality obligations survive for a period of one (1) year. Even though they state the same, the Change Text is what modified it to cause a contradiction, therefore, the Change Text introduces a clear inconsistency within the contract, making it disputable in a court of law, as per the explanation given.
